steps.title: Keep the appositive intact
steps.content: The blank names the rare carbon isotope and gives its abbreviation, carbon-13 (<sup>13</sup>C). That whole appositive must stay together, then a comma is needed before the sentence continues with "due to."

steps.title: Place the comma correctly
steps.content: A and C wrongly insert a comma between the isotope name and its abbreviation. B keeps the unit intact but lacks the closing comma. The correct punctuation keeps carbon-13 (13C) together and sets it off from what follows. The answer is D.
